name: Strong Towns Tax Explorer
description: A map of property tax data for the city of Madison, built with DSSD for Strong Towns' Madison chapter. I led the team of the maps and statistics pages, learning from my past work on DSSD's Red CORAL project.

name: .ddw Theme Creator
description: A modernized fork of a tool for creating custom dynamic desktop wallpapers for Win Dynamic Desktop.

name: Minecraft Fractal
description: A zoomable experiment where Minecraft textures recursively replace pixels for an infinite fractal effect.

name: Paintball
description: An alternative frontend for multiple image generation models where you can bring your own API keys.

name: Restaurant Receipt Splitter
description: Scans a restaurant receipt, lets you assign entrees to people, then calculates tip and tax for a clean split.

name: SimpleGIF
description: A streamlined tool for creating, converting, cropping, and compressing GIFs.

name: Super Brothers Reader
description: A lightweight reader for the Super Brothers webcomic designed to avoid the friction of using DeviantArt.

name: Trakt iCal Generator
description: Generates an iCal feed from your Trakt calendar so you can track movie and show release dates.

name: Bucky's Buzzer Beater
description: Kahoot, but Jeopardy. A real-time trivia game built with websockets and Rust(!?) for MadHacks 2025.

name: WebLabs Operation Weave
description: The second workshop event site for WebLabs' WebFest series, teaching participants SQL, REST APIs, and LLM tool calls through hands-on challenges.

name: Bike Benefits Map
description: A map of all businesses in the US that offer Bike Benefits discounts, optimized for mobile.

name: Vibe Check
description: Creates an API key that lets an LLM agent share your location and Discord status.

name: CS559 Showcase
description: The class project showcase site for CS559: Computer Graphics at UW-Madison.

name: Fishbowl
description: A 3D fishbowl simulation built with pure WebGL. Loads in models and handwritten shaders! My final project for CS559: Computer Graphics at UW-Madison.

name: DSSD Madison - Red CORAL
description: An open-source club project that I led. A web app for a non-profit in South America that filters, sorts, and visualizes crime data on a map, table, and other charts.

name: LinkLooker
description: My first browser extension. Previews links using ChatGPT when you hover over them in Chrome and Firefox.

name: GitHub Discord Previews
description: A project that embeds Discord statuses in SVGs that can be easily placed anywhere. Built with an HTML frontend and React backend(!?).

name: ImageHound
description: My interview take-home for Fetch, where I interned during the summer of 2025. A web app that displays dog images from an API and lets you select and search for specfiic breeds, all coded in a handful of hours in Svelte.

name: Staff Discord Bot
description: A Discord bot that accesses the Splatoon 3 API to get information about the game, like current shop and map rotations. It also let discord members vote for their favorite Splatfest teams.

name: Back End Development and APIs
description: freeCodeCamp certification in backend development and APIs.

name: Front End Web Development
description: freeCodeCamp responsive web design certification.

name: JS Algorithms and Data Structures
description: freeCodeCamp JavaScript algorithms and data structures certification.

name: Front End Development Libraries
description: freeCodeCamp front end development libraries certification.

name: Data Visualization
description: freeCodeCamp data visualization certification.

name: Mark Neumann for Congress
description: A Wordpress site for a local political campaign

name: Portfolio Website
description: The website you're currently on! Built with Astro, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript.
